grant_title: Illegal Disposal Site Abatement Grant Program (Cycle 104/106)
administering_agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery
subcontractor_eligibility: YES - public entities hire contractors for cleanup work
eligible_service_lines: Illegal dumping cleanup / solid waste remediation / debris removal
required_certifications: None for contractor; public entity must apply
reporting_burden: Moderate - final report and payment request by June 30 2028
reimbursement_structure: Reimbursement grant - costs incurred then reimbursed
scoring_criteria_notes: Competitive - typically $2M-$5M in requests vs $1M available
strategic_priority: HIGH - Prime Pathwy as subcontractor to public entity applicants
notes: STRATEGY: Partner with Solano/Sacramento/Alameda/Contra Costa/San Joaquin counties as their cleanup contractor. County applies for grant; Prime Pathwy performs the work. This is a direct revenue path.

grant_title: Farm and Ranch Solid Waste Cleanup and Abatement Grant Program (Cycle 91)
administering_agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery
eligible_service_lines: Farm/ranch debris cleanup / solid waste removal
notes: STRATEGY: Same as above - partner with county agricultural departments. Solano and San Joaquin counties have significant agricultural land.

administering_agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery
contractor_eligibility: YES - manufacturers using recycled crumb rubber
eligible_service_lines: Tire-derived product manufacturing / crumb rubber use
reimbursement_structure: Incentive payment per pound of crumb rubber used
notes: NOTE: Prime Pathwy would need to manufacture tire-derived products to qualify. More relevant as a subcontractor to tire processors. Monitor for tire COLLECTION opportunities.

grant_title: Tire Derived Aggregate Grant Program (TDA) - FY 2026-27 Cycle 28
administering_agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery
contractor_eligibility: YES - eligible applicants include businesses using TDA
eligible_service_lines: Tire-derived aggregate use in construction/civil engineering

administering_agency: EPA Office of Brownfields and Land Revitalization
contractor_eligibility: No - government/nonprofit entities only; contractors hired for cleanup work
subcontractor_eligibility: YES - eligible entities hire private contractors for remediation
eligible_service_lines: Environmental remediation / brownfield cleanup / hazardous substance removal
required_certifications: None for contractor; entity must apply
scoring_criteria_notes: Community benefit / site contamination severity / redevelopment plan
notes: STRATEGY: Identify brownfield sites in target counties. Partner with city/county redevelopment agencies applying for EPA Brownfields grants. Prime Pathwy performs the cleanup work.

contractor_eligibility: No - state/local governments apply; contractors hired
subcontractor_eligibility: YES - local governments hire contractors for mitigation work
eligible_service_lines: Debris removal / hazard mitigation / disaster cleanup
strategic_priority: HIGH - disaster response is immediate revenue
notes: STRATEGY: Register in SAM.gov Disaster Response Registry. Register with Army Corps of Engineers Contractor Registry. Build relationships with CalOES and county emergency managers. LA Wildfire DR-4856-CA declared Jan 2025 - monitor for NorCal disaster declarations.

grant_title: FEMA Public Assistance - Debris Removal (Category A)
contractor_eligibility: No - government entities apply; contractors hired
subcontractor_eligibility: YES - government entities hire local contractors
eligible_service_lines: Debris removal / disaster cleanup / environmental services
notes: STRATEGY: Register in SAM.gov Disaster Response Registry NOW. Submit FEMA Vendor Profile Form. Build local government relationships in all 5 target counties. When disaster strikes, local vendors are preferred. LA Wildfire 2025 is proof of concept.

subcontractor_eligibility: None - this is a LOAN not a grant
reporting_burden: Any small business affected by disaster
notes: MEDIUM - capital access for disaster-affected operations
column23: NOTE: This is a LOAN program not a grant. Useful for capital access after disaster impacts Prime Pathwy operations. Current: SBA offering relief for 2025 Late December Storms and 2026 Drought.

grant_title: California IBank Small Business Loan Guarantee Program
program_source: California Infrastructure and Economic Development Bank
reimbursement_structure: SBA small business size standards; California-based
strategic_priority: Loan guarantee (reduces interest rate and collateral requirements)
source_url: Credit history / business plan / job creation
notes: HIGH - capital access for equipment and fleet
column23: STRATEGY: Use IBank guarantee to finance equipment purchases (trucks, cleaning equipment) at lower rates. Reduces capital barriers for scaling operations.

program_source: GO-Biz / California Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development
contractor_eligibility: No - nonprofits and local health departments only
application_deadline: Phase 2 deadline was December 3 2025; FY 2026-27 TBD
geographic_scope: California statewide - focus on underserved communities
eligible_service_lines: Job placement / mental health / community services
required_certifications: 501(c)(3) nonprofit status required for direct application
strategic_priority: LOW DIRECT - but HIGH for partnership
notes: NOTE: Prime Pathwy cannot apply directly. STRATEGY: Partner with community nonprofits in Vallejo/Richmond/Antioch who receive CalCRG funds and need cleanup/facility services as part of their programs.

program_source: California Office of the Small Business Advocate
subcontractor_eligibility: N/A - TA program not direct business grants
eligible_service_lines: Small business technical assistance and support
strategic_priority: LOW DIRECT - but access free TA services
notes: NOTE: Prime Pathwy should ACCESS services from CalOSBA TA providers (SBDC/APEX) not apply for this grant. Free business advising is available.

grant_title: Household Hazardous Waste Grant Program (Cycle 45) - FY 2025-26
administering_agency: California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery
eligible_service_lines: Household hazardous waste collection and disposal
notes: STRATEGY: Partner with Solano/Sacramento/Contra Costa/Alameda/San Joaquin counties as HHW collection event contractor.
